key_themes: Consistent dividend growth as a Dividend King
Improving US foodservice local case volumes and gross margins
Full-year adjusted EPS guidance raised to high end of range
Analyst Buy consensus with 15-20% average price target upside
Strategic moves including Restaurant Depot acquisition and local sourcing initiatives
summary: Sysco delivered steady Q2 FY2026 results with 3% sales growth, positive 1.2% local case volume growth, gross margin expansion, and raised its full-year adjusted EPS guidance to the high end of $4.50–$4.60. The company announced a 1.9% quarterly dividend increase to $0.55 (extending its 57-year streak as a Dividend King), which has generated positive buzz among income investors. Analysts maintain a Buy/Moderate Buy consensus with average targets near $88 (15–20% upside from recent ~$74–76 levels), though growth remains moderate amid ongoing industry volume and margin pressures.
sources: 15+ web news articles and analyst reports (Q2 FY2026 earnings, dividend hike, guidance, forecasts), 10+ recent X posts (primarily dividend announcement reactions)
